verbGlossary.DECOMPOSES INTO: Parent {target} breaks down into individual line items that allocate dollars to specific scopes of work

verbGlossary.GROUPED BY: {target} is tagged with a budget code for executive-level reporting rollups
verbGlossary.MEASURED BY: {source}'s performance is quantified by a standard metric or KPI

verbGlossary.DRAWS FROM: {target} pulls funds from contingency reserve to cover approved scope changes
verbGlossary.SUBDIVIDED BY: {target} is further broken into WBS segments for building-element-level cost tracking within a trade
verbGlossary.ITEMIZED INTO: {source}is broken into cost-code-level detail rows for granular tracking
verbGlossary.STRUCTURED BY: {target} billing framework is defined by a schedule of values with discrete billable line items
verbGlossary.BILLED THROUGH: {source}bills against {target} via periodic invoices referencing the subcontract SOV
verbGlossary.WITHHOLDS: {target} specifies a retainage percentage held back from each progress payment until completion
verbGlossary.CHARGES AGAINST: {source} transaction posts actual cost against {target}, reducing available budget
verbGlossary.CODED TO: {target} is assigned a cost code that maps the expenditure to a specific trade or work category
verbGlossary.TAGGED WITH: {target} carries a WBS code that identifies the building element, phase, or location of the cost
verbGlossary.BROKEN DOWN INTO: {source}is decomposed into cost-code-level {target}s for budget alignment
verbGlossary.ADVANCES TO: {target} progresses to a potential change order that targets a specific subcontract for modification
verbGlossary.MATURES INTO: {target} lifecycle progresses to a formal commitment change order when the change is approved and priced
verbGlossary.AGGREGATED INTO: Multiple {target}s are combined into a single owner change order for prime contract modification
verbGlossary.CONVERTS TO: Potential {target} is approved and becomes a formal commitment change order modifying the subcontract
verbGlossary.MODIFIES: Potential {target} proposes a modification to the scope and cost of an existing commitment
verbGlossary.REVISES: Approved {target} change order updates the committed amount on the parent subcontract or purchase order
verbGlossary.ADJUSTS: {target} change order modifies the dollar amount on existing commitment line items
verbGlossary.IMPACTS: CCO {target} changes the committed cost charged to a budget line item, affecting variance and forecast
verbGlossary.AMENDS: Owner {target} adds new line items or modifies existing amounts on the schedule of values
verbGlossary.BILLABLE THROUGH: Approved owner {target} scope becomes eligible for billing through subsequent pay applications
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS: Owner {target} approval triggers a budget modification to reallocate funds across budget lines
verbGlossary.FUNDS: {target} reserve provides the funding source for approved change events that exceed original budget
verbGlossary.SPENT THROUGH: {source} is consumed vi{target}s or commitments as undefined scope is clarified and priced
verbGlossary.COMMITTED VIA: {source}are locked into a formal subcontract when the undefined scope is defined and awarded
verbGlossary.DRIVES: {target} line items determine the billing amounts on each periodic invoice or pay application
verbGlossary.ACCRUES: Each {target} billing cycle adds to the cumulative retainage balance withheld from earned payments
verbGlossary.SETTLES THROUGH: Approved {target} amount is disbursed to the payee via a payment transaction after processing
verbGlossary.FORECASTED IN: Owner {target} amounts and timing are projected in the cash flow model for draw scheduling
verbGlossary.REQUIRES: Each {target} transaction requires an executed lien waiver before funds are released
verbGlossary.RELEASED THROUGH: Accumulated {target} is disbursed via a payment at substantial or final completion milestones
verbGlossary.PRODUCES: {target} calculation generates the estimate at completion variance metric each reporting period

verbGlossary.DRIVEN BY: {source}cost growth percentage is substantially influenced by the cumulative {target} rate
verbGlossary.SOURCED FROM: {source}cost metric aggregates the total contract value from subcontracts and purchase orders
verbGlossary.EXPRESSED AS: {source} absolute dollar amount is converted to {target} percentage for normalization and benchmarking
verbGlossary.TRACKED OVER TIME BY: {source} is monitored across reporting periods by {target} to detect erosion trends
verbGlossary.ERODED BY: {source} is diminished when {target} increases — each unrecovered change reduces projected profit
verbGlossary.APPLIED TO: {source} percentage is added on top of {target} cost to determine the billing or sell price
verbGlossary.CONTRIBUTES TO: {source} markup revenue flows through to {target} when actual costs stay within estimates
verbGlossary.FUTURE SOURCE OF: {source} represents unearned contract value that will generate {target} as work is completed
verbGlossary.FEEDS: Higher {source} produces more contract wins that build {target} over time
verbGlossary.TRADEOFF WITH: {source} and {target} have an inverse relationship — aggressive pricing wins more work at lower margins
verbGlossary.IMPROVES: Higher {source} reduces pursuit cost and increases {target} because repeat clients require less competitive bidding
verbGlossary.CORRELATES WITH: {source} and {target} tend to move together — repeat clients typically produce more predictable margins
verbGlossary.RISK DIMENSION OF: {source} measures the portfolio diversification risk embedded in {target} composition
verbGlossary.PROJECTS: {source} forward-looking estimate determines the projected {target} at completion
verbGlossary.COMPRESSES: Higher {source} directly reduces {target} by consuming revenue before it reaches profit
verbGlossary.REDUCES: {source} non-project costs reduce {target} dollar amount available to the company
verbGlossary.CONSTRAINS: {source} limitation restricts {target} capacity or growth potential
verbGlossary.MEASURES CYCLE TIME OF: {source} quantifies the elapsed time from {target} initiation to completion
verbGlossary.MEASURES COLLECTION OF: {source} tracks the average collection period from {target} submission to cash receipt
verbGlossary.SECURED BY: {source} surety bond secures the contractor's performance obligation on {target}
verbGlossary.LEADING INDICATOR OF: Rising {source} predicts future deterioration in {target} within 30–60 days
